events.title: Lebanon ‘dragged back into turmoil’, UN envoy warns
events.description: Lebanon has been “dragged back into a state of turmoil and violence”, the UN’s top envoy in the country warned on Saturday, after the latest round of regional strikes triggered a fast‑escalating crisis along the Blue Line. What had been fragile but real momentum, she said, has now collapsed in a matter of days.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon ‘dragged back into turmoil’, UN envoy warns

events.title: At least 65 Nigerian soldiers killed in jihadist raids in country’s north-east
events.description: Gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province overran four military bases and abducted 300 civilians, say reports At least 65 Nigerian soldiers have been killed in jihadist raids across the country’s north-east in the last two weeks, as the west African state battles to contain one of the world’s deadliest terror groups. On 5 and 6 March, gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province (Iswap) overran four military bases in Borno state, the epicentre of the insurgency. Nigerian daily the Punch reported that about 40 soldiers were killed in total in these attacks. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 65 Nigerian soldiers killed in jihadist raids in country’s north-east

events.title: 'EU needs a bit of a revolution: There is no European strategy or foreign policy'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Sophie in 't Veld, former Dutch MEP and member of Volt Europa. She argues that the EU faces a profound structural crisis in its capacity to act on the global stage. What appears as policy debate is, in practice, an internal academic discussion with little consequence for international affairs. In moments of crisis, the EU is not shaping events but reacting to them, often in response to the initiatives of other powers. Ms. Veld warns that Europe has become largely irrelevant in global geopolitics.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'EU needs a bit of a revolution: There is no European strategy or foreign policy'

events.title: Japan marks 15 years since tsunami as Takaichi pushes more nuclear energy use
events.description: Japan is marking the 15th anniversary of the earthquake, tsunami and nuclear disaster on its northeastern coast as the government pushes for atomic energy use
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan marks 15 years since tsunami as Takaichi pushes more nuclear energy use

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 March: Displacement, supply shocks and civilian toll rise
events.description: The conflict in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counter-strikes across the region and the humanitarian toll mounting. UN agencies warn that rising civilian casualties, mass displacement – particularly in Lebanon – and damage to critical infrastructure are deepening the crisis, while disruptions to shipping and energy routes risk driving up global prices and worsening food insecurity. Stay with us for updates from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 March: Displacement, supply shocks and civilian toll rise

events.title: Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight
events.description: From fuel caps to four-day work weeks, the Middle East conflict has left the world’s top crude oil importing region desperate to shore up supplies Donald Trump has scrambled in recent days to reassure the world that the economic impact of his war on Iran can be contained. Sure, one of the most important waterways in global trade has, in effect, been shut for almost two weeks – but it might reopen before long. In the meantime, US oil-related sanctions on “some countries” will be lifted. And besides, the entire conflict could be over soon . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight

events.title: Iranian regime-linked handler investigated by Australian police over alleged death threat
events.description: Protester alleges that Iranian handler ‘turned around and looked at us and then drew a line on his throat and pointed to us’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian police investigated a complaint involving an Iranian regime-linked handler for the country’s football team after a group of activists alleged he threatened to kill them at a Women’s Asian Cup match last week. The criminal investigation, confirmed by Queensland police, has led to renewed calls from the Iranian diaspora for tougher immigration screening processes to block people linked to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) – a proscribed state sponsor of terrorism – from entering the country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iranian regime-linked handler investigated by Australian police over alleged death threat

events.title: Quit fossil fuels to stem deadly floods in Brazil’s coffee heartland, say scientists
events.description: Global heating linked to rising risk of extreme rain that causes devastating landslides and rising coffee prices The record floods that have brought death and destruction to the heartland of Brazil’s coffee industry are expected to intensify if people continue to burn fossil fuels, analysis has shown. Dozens of residents in the state of Minas Gerais have been buried alive in landslides or swept away as roads turned into rivers over the past month. Thousands more have been forced to evacuate their homes, while the wider, longer-term effects are likely to include higher prices for coffee across the world. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Quit fossil fuels to stem deadly floods in Brazil’s coffee heartland, say scientists

events.title: Iran may tap into organised crime to carry out terror attacks, expert says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Graig Klein, Assistant Professor in Terrorism and Political Violence at Leiden University in The Hague, explains that Iran may tap into organised crime in order to carry out terror attacks in Europe in retaliation for the war waged by the US and Israel, adding that Donald Trump is being complacent about the threat as the latest incidents targeting US embassies abroad "is part of the Iranian strategic playbook".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran may tap into organised crime to carry out terror attacks, expert says

events.title: At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan
events.description: Strike in Shukeiri killed schoolgirls, teachers and healthcare workers in latest incident in three-year war At least 17 people, most of them schoolgirls, were killed on Wednesday when an explosive-laden drone blamed on Sudan’ s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces struck a secondary school and a health care centre. At least 10 people were wounded in the strike in the village of Shukeiri in the White Nile province, according to Dr Musa al-Majeri, director of Douiem hospital, the nearest major medical facility to the village. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan

events.title: Iran regime cited as Trump admin set to designate Sudan’s Muslim Brotherhood a terror group
events.description: Trump administration designates Sudanese Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organization amid Sudan's civil war that has seen thousands of deaths and millions of displacements.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran regime cited as Trump admin set to designate Sudan’s Muslim Brotherhood a terror group

events.title: Drone strike reportedly kills at least 1 person in the eastern Congo city of Goma
events.description: A series of explosions attributed to drones has killed at least one person as the attack rocked downtown Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone strike reportedly kills at least 1 person in the eastern Congo city of Goma

events.title: LIVE: International Women’s Day and CSW70 at the United Nations
events.description: Welcome to our live coverage of International Women’s Day 2026 and the opening of the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women at UN Headquarters in New York. Throughout the day, we will bring you updates, reactions and key moments from global leaders, diplomats and advocates gathering at the UN, alongside stories and reports from the field across the UN system, as communities around the world mark International Women's Day and advance the theme “Rights. Justice. Action” for all women and girls. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.

events.title: Norway arrests three brothers over US embassy bombing in Oslo
events.description: Norway police on Wednesday said they had arrested three brothers suspected of a "terrorist bombing" outside the US Embassy in Oslo that caused minor damage but no injuries, as investigators probed possible motives including links to Iran.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Norway arrests three brothers over US embassy bombing in Oslo

events.title: How the Iran conflict is becoming a global crisis
events.description: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar airstrikes worsen crisis, South Sudan fighting threatens Akobo, Afghan women shut out of justice
events.description: Conflict continues to fuel suffering and drive humanitarian needs in Myanmar, the UN relief coordination office, OCHA, said in a bulletin on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Myanmar airstrikes worsen crisis, South Sudan fighting threatens Akobo, Afghan women shut out of justice

events.title: A world war? Iran war draws in more than regional rivals
events.description: One war can fuel another. After the United States and Israel launched an operation that began, on day one, with the assassination of Iran’s supreme leader, Donald Trump expressed surprise at Tehran’s decision to draw in Gulf oil producers and disrupt global supplies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A world war? Iran war draws in more than regional rivals

events.title: 3 brothers of Iraqi descent arrested in Oslo for allegedly 'terror bombing' of US Embassy
events.description: Three Norwegian brothers of Iraqi origin were arrested for allegedly bombing the U.S. Embass in Oslo. No injuries were reported in Sunday's explosive attack.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 brothers of Iraqi descent arrested in Oslo for allegedly 'terror bombing' of US Embassy

events.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
events.description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war

events.title: Middle East war live: Iranian army says it targeted Israeli military bases and security service
events.description: Iran's army on Thursday said it had targeted Israeli military bases and the country's security service Shin Bet as the war entered its 13th day. Earlier, an Israeli strike on central Beirut's seafront killed at least seven people in the heart of the capital as Iran-backed Hezbollah launched more missiles at Israel. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: Iranian army says it targeted Israeli military bases and security service

events.title: There's a dangerous new depth to warfare in Sudan - and it's a death trap for civilians
events.description: A drone is circling in the skies above Zaki Ramadan when he takes my call. As a humanitarian worker in Sudan's southeastern Blue Nile state, he is helplessly watching aid dwindle and civilians be killed by the meteoric rise in drone strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 There's a dangerous new depth to warfare in Sudan - and it's a death trap for civilians

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues to reverberate across the region and beyond. Oil prices have climbed to around $100 a barrel as attacks on shipping and energy infrastructure disrupt key supply routes. Strikes and counterstrikes between Israel, Hezbollah and Iran continue, while ships have reportedly been hit in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman, raising fears of wider shocks to global markets. Meanwhile, UN agencies are expanding humanitarian relief efforts as displacement rises and civilian needs grow across the region. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets
events.description: As the conflict in the Middle East continues to ripple across the region, the UN Security Council met today to discuss the escalating situation, with a morning briefing focused on Lebanon and a second session later on the wider crisis – adopting a Bahrain-led resolution, while a Russian-led text failed to secure enough votes. Across the UN system, agencies are warning of mounting humanitarian needs as displacement rises, services come under strain and regional instability disrupts mobility, trade and aid operations. Stay with us as we bring you the latest developments, reactions and updates from UN Headquarters and across the region. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets

events.title: US war on Iran: Using military force to achieve political outcomes rarely produces lasting stability
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dafydd Townley, Teaching Fellow in US politics and International Security at the University of Portsmouth. For Mr. Townley, the most striking feature of the current US operation against Iran is the gap between clear military action and ambiguous political strategy. The Pentagon has articulated core military objectives, which includes weakening the country’s military. What remains far less clear, however, is the political end game that these operations are meant to achieve.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US war on Iran: Using military force to achieve political outcomes rarely produces lasting stability

events.title: ‘I left everything for my grandchildren’: In Beirut, displaced families exhausted by endless war
events.description: More than 700,000 people have been forced to leave their homes in Lebanon since the country was dragged back into war with Israel on March 2. FRANCE 24’s reporter met with displaced families who have found refuge in Beirut’s overwhelmed emergency shelters, and others who have no choice but to sleep out in the streets of the Lebanese capital.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘I left everything for my grandchildren’: In Beirut, displaced families exhausted by endless war

events.title: Three merchant ships struck as tensions rise in Hormuz strait amid Iran war
events.description: Crew of Thai-registered bulk carrier forced to flee fire, as US says it has destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels Middle East crisis live – latest updates Three merchant ships have been struck in and around the strait of Hormuz, including a Thai registered bulk carrier that caught fire after leaving a port in the UAE, forcing crew members to evacuate for their safety. The Mayuree Naree was struck on Wednesday by “two projectiles of unknown origin”, its owners said, as it sailed about 11 nautical miles north of Oman, marking the end of a four-day lull of attacks in the strategic waterway. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three merchant ships struck as tensions rise in Hormuz strait amid Iran war

events.title: Meta disables more than 150,000 accounts in crackdown on south-east Asian scam networks
events.description: Company also launches tools to spot scammers as Thai police arrest 21 people Meta disabled more than 150,000 accounts and Thai police arrested 21 people in a sweeping international crackdown on south-east Asian criminal scam centers that targeted people around the world, the social media company said on Wednesday. The operation was led by Thailand ’s Royal Thai police anti-cyber scam center, alongside the FBI and the US justice department’s scam center strike force, with Meta investigators acting on intelligence shared in real time by law enforcement. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Meta disables more than 150,000 accounts in crackdown on south-east Asian scam networks

events.title: Iran's new supreme leader injured in strikes which killed his father
events.description: Iran’s new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the US-Israeli airstrikes which killed his father.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's new supreme leader injured in strikes which killed his father

events.title: An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased
events.description: Around two-thirds of children worldwide report an increase in cyberbullying, with one in two say they don’t know how to get the right support, according to a recent poll carried out by the UN’s top official who works to end violence against children.
events.ticker_label: 💥 An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased

events.title: US diplomatic facility in Iraq struck by drone

events.title: Queensland’s ‘from the river to the sea’ laws likened to Bjelke-Petersen era anti-protest regime
events.description: Health minister Tim Nicholls corrects claim alleged Bondi terror attack gunman Naveed Akram used controversial phrase, as laws roundly criticised Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police arresting pro-Palestinian protesters for using the phrase “from the river to the sea” had “all the hallmarks of an authoritarian police state”, according to a Greens MP, amid widespread backlash against Queensland’s new hate speech laws. The controversial laws went into effect yesterday after passing a vote in parliament last week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Queensland’s ‘from the river to the sea’ laws likened to Bjelke-Petersen era anti-protest regime

events.title: New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation
events.description: Despite some progress made globally, children around the world still face serious and evolving risks of being sold, sexually exploited and abused, the UN independent human rights expert on the sale, sexual exploitation and sexual abuse of children, Mama Fatima Singhateh, warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation

events.title: Canada’s Carney under pressure to act after synagogues shot at in latest antisemitic incidents
events.description: Gunfire attacks on Toronto synagogues highlight Canada's rising antisemitism crisis, as hate incidents against the country's Jewish community continues.

events.title: Rubio designates Afghanistan as 'state sponsor of wrongful detention': 'Despicable tactics'
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio designated Afghanistan as a state sponsor of wrongful detention, accusing the Taliban of using terrorist tactics against Americans.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Rubio designates Afghanistan as 'state sponsor of wrongful detention': 'Despicable tactics'

events.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 13 of US-Israel attacks?
events.description: Cyberattacks, tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and rising oil prices are deepening the global consequences of the war.

events.title: French aid worker among three killed in dronestrike in east DRC, M23 rebels say
events.description: Rebel group blames government for attack on residential area of M23-controlled city of Goma Three people including a French UN aid worker have been killed in a drone attack in Goma, a spokesperson for the M23 rebel group has said. The attack took place at about 4am on Wednesday in the upmarket residential neighbourhood of Himbi in the city, which has been under M23 occupation since January 2025. Continue reading...

events.title: The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis

events.title: Threats to the Strait of Hormuz raises concerns about global oil prices
events.description: The threat of Iran laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz -- the narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil supply travels -- is raising fresh fears about global oil prices.

events.title: Private security firm helping Americans evacuate the Middle East amid war with Iran
events.description: A global security firm based in Virginia says it has evacuated more than 4,000 people from the Middle East since the U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Private security firm helping Americans evacuate the Middle East amid war with Iran

events.description: The Iran conflict is driving up energy and fertilizer costs, raising alarms over a return of food inflation. Farmers are bracing for resource shortages that could drag down harvests.

events.title: Has Cambodia really cut cybercrime by half?
events.description: Cambodia claims to be finally dismantling the cyber-scam compounds that have made the country a global fraud hub. Analysts warn there is little proof that top figures in the scam industry will face consequences.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Has Cambodia really cut cybercrime by half?

events.title: Banking, finance and consultancy firms evacuate staff in Gulf States
events.description: Several major international businesses closed or evacuated offices in Gulf countries after Iran threatened US- and Israel-linked economic targets and centres in the Middle East. US finance group Citi and British consultancy Deloitte evacuated offices in Dubai's financial centre, while PwC, another British consultancy, closed offices in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE and Kuwait. HSBC has closed all branches in Qatar until further notice.

events.title: Germany, Austria to release oil from reserves in response to 400M barrel request
events.description: Germany and Austria say they will release parts of their oil reserves following an International Energy Agency request for members to release a record 400 million barrels to help calm energy price spikes due to the Iran war
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany, Austria to release oil from reserves in response to 400M barrel request

events.title: World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees

events.title: Drone strike kills French aid worker and civilians in Goma
events.description: In tonight’s edition: A French aid worker is killed by a drone strike in Goma, in eastern DRC. Also, global cocoa prices have plunged after 2024’s record highs, forcing producers to cut payments to farmers. And, after nearly 170 years, leopards are returning to South Africa’s western coast – a real conservation success.

events.title: 'Two decades in the making': expert details how Mossad built its network of spies in Iran
events.description: Iran has ⁠arrested dozens including ​a foreign national for allegedly spying for the country's "enemies". Dr Aviva Guttmann, Lecturer in Strategy and Intelligence at the Department of International Politics at Aberystwyth University, details how Mossad has inflirtated Iran's security apparatus over the past two decades. She also explains that Iranian intelligence is resorting to using "disposable agents" for its operations in Israel.

events.title: ‘No one should be a hibakusha’: Young Japanese activist’s mission to share atomic bomb survivor stories

events.title: Teen charged in senior prank gone wrong makes vow after teacher’s death

events.title: UN commission opens with focus on justice for women and girls
events.description: Access to justice for women and girls dominated the agenda on Monday as the Commission on the Status of Women opened its 70th session at UN Headquarters in New York. Government officials, civil society representatives and UN leaders called for renewed efforts to dismantle the discrimination and legal obstacles that continue to limit women’s and girls’ rights worldwide.

events.title: Nearly 700,000 displaced in Lebanon as Middle East crisis escalates
events.description: On day 10 of the war engulfing the Middle East, UN agencies on Monday reported massive displacement across the region, along with surging food and fuel prices that risk increasing hunger and suffering for the most vulnerable.

events.title: Iran's soccer team cannot participate in the FIFA World Cup, Iranian minister says
events.description: Iran is set to play three games in the U.S. this June. But amid the U.S.-Israel military campaign that has killed Iran's supreme leader, Iran's sports minister said the team would pull out.

events.title: Extraordinary announcement not enough to bring down oil price
events.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA), the developed nation body founded in the 1970s to deal with oil crises precisely like the one we're now facing, has announced something extraordinary.

events.title: ‘Toxic rain’ warning from oil depot strikes amid ongoing Middle East war
events.description: Toxic “black rain” linked to strikes on oil depots, mass displacement and continuing disruption to aid supply chains are upending lives across the Middle East and beyond after 10 days of war in the region, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.

events.description: The attack appears to be another Israeli attempted assassination, outside areas traditionally targeted.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli ‘double-tap strike’ hits displaced on Beirut seafront, kills eight

events.title: ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts
events.description: Countries across the continent have spent more than $2bn on Chinese tracking technology that is not ‘necessary or proportionate’, new report finds The rapid expansion of AI-powered mass-surveillance systems across Africa is violating citizens’ right to privacy and having a chilling effect on society, according to experts on human rights and emerging technologies. At least $2bn (£1.5bn) has been spent by 11 African governments on Chinese-built surveillance technology that recognises faces and monitors movements, according to a new report by the Institute of Development Studies, which warns that national security is being used to justify implementing these systems with little regulation. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts

events.title: Haiti president’s assassination driven by greed and power, US prosecutors say
events.description: Opening statements begin in Miami trial of four men accused in the 2021 killing of Jovenel Moïse Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Greed, arrogance and power were the driving forces behind four men charged in the US for the 2021 assassination of Haiti’s last elected president, Jovenel Moïse , prosecutors told a court on Tuesday during opening statements. Federal prosecutors and defense attorneys began presenting opening statements in the trial in Miami for Arcangel Pretel Ortíz, Antonio Intriago, Walter Veintemilla and James Solages. They are charged with conspiring in south Florida to kidnap or kill Haiti’s former leader. Moïse’s assassination led to unprecedented turmoil in the Caribbean nation, where gang leaders have grown increasingly violent and empowered. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran war: Oil prices jump as Iran steps up tanker attacks
events.description: Iran is continuing to target energy infrastructure across the Gulf. Iraq paused operations at all of its oil ports after an attack on two tankers and Oman has evacuated all ships from its main export terminal. Follow DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Oil prices jump as Iran steps up tanker attacks

events.title: Why South Africa is sending soldiers into cities

events.title: Israel launches new 'large-scale' wave of strikes on south Beirut
events.description: The Israeli military said Wednesday that it had launched a new wave of strikes on Hezbollah targets in the southern Beirut suburbs, vowing to act with "great force" in the area. The military said it had begun a "large-scale wave of strikes" on Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahiyeh, the Lebanese capital's southern suburb, where the Iran-backed group holds sway. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports from Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel launches new 'large-scale' wave of strikes on south Beirut

events.title: A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?
events.description: Viral video of girl being shoved by fellow pedestrian has reignited debate over butsukari – with experts blaming stress and gender dynamics It starts out as a heartwarming clip. A young girl, clearly delighted to be in Tokyo, beams as she makes a peace sign to the camera. Seconds later, she is shoved to the ground from behind by a woman wearing a surgical mask. The assailant doesn’t skip a beat, striding out of shot of the clip filmed by the girl’s mother. This was no accidental clash of shoulders in a crowded place, but one of the most visible examples of a spate of b utsukari otoko – “bumping man” – shoving incidents in Japan that experts attribute to a combination of gender dynamics and the stresses of modern life. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?

events.title: ‘Beggars belief’: calls for federal intervention after extension to ‘carbon bomb’ open-cut coalmine approved by Queensland government
events.description: Hail Creek is responsible for about 20% of Australia’s coalmine methane, while only producing 1% of the country’s coal Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Environmental groups have called on the federal government to intervene after Australia’s most methane-polluting open-cut coalmine was approved for extension. A proposed expansion of the Hail Creek coalmine in central Queensland, described by conservationists as a “carbon bomb”, was backed by the state’s government on Wednesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘Beggars belief’: calls for federal intervention after extension to ‘carbon bomb’ open-cut coalmine approved by Queensland government

events.title: Argentina grants asylum to Brasília rioter in move that may sway Brazil vote
events.description: Decision to shield pro-Bolsonaro truck driver sentenced for 8 January 2023 attack could inflame Brazil election politics Argentina has granted asylum to a Brazilian fugitive convicted for his role in 2023 pro-Bolsonaro riots – a decision that analysts say could reverberate in Brazil ’s upcoming presidential election. A week after Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Brazil’s president, took office, hundreds of people ransacked Brazil’s congress building, presidential palace and supreme court on 8 January 2023, in an attempt to overturn former president Jair Bolsonaro’s electoral defeat. Investigators later concluded the attacks were the culmination of a broader plot aimed at staging a coup . Continue reading...

events.title: China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target minorities, say rights groups
events.description: China approved a new "ethnic unity" law on Thursday, which formalises policies to promote Mandarin as the "national common language" in education, official business and public places. China's government has been accused for decades of pursuing policies to force assimilation across the vast country into the Han majority, and rights groups believe the new law could further marginalise minority groups.

events.description: Tehran may seek to choke off oil traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz following US and Israeli strikes on Iran, raising fears it could deploy sea mines, officials say. President Donald Trump said US forces struck 28 Iranian mine-laying vessels as concerns grow over threats to the strategic shipping lane.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s sea mines raise fears of Strait of Hormuz oil disruption

events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: China’s rubber-stamp parliament set to approve ‘ethnic unity’ law
events.description: New legislation will require schools to use Mandarin by default, taking priority over minority ethnic languages such as Tibetan, Uyghur and Mongolian China’s National People’s Congress (NPC), the state legislature, will vote on Thursday on a suite of new laws agreed at this year’s annual two sessions gathering , including a piece of legislation that will diminish the role of minority ethnic languages in the education system. NPC delegates are expected to approve a new ethnic unity law, along with a new environmental code and the 15th five-year plan, the economic planning document for 2026-2030. Delegates have spent the last week debating Beijing’s proposed bills, which they are all but certain to approve. The NPC, which is often described as a rubber-stamp parliament, has never rejected an item on its agenda. Continue reading...

events.title: Experts fear ‘unethical’ vaccine trial in Africa is ‘prototype’ for US studies under RFK Jr
events.description: Danish researchers whose work on effects of vaccines has been called into question are at center of US vaccine policy New details are leading experts to fear that an “unethical” vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau is the “prototype” for studies under Robert F Kennedy Jr, secretary of the US department of health and human services (HHS) and longtime vaccine critic. At the center of US vaccine policy is an unlikely set of Danish researchers whose work on the health effects of vaccines has been called into question. The study in Guinea-Bissau would have looked at the overall health effects of giving hepatitis B vaccines by only vaccinating half of the newborns in the study at birth despite an 18% prevalence rate in adults of the illness, which can lead to serious and sometimes fatal health consequences. Continue reading...

events.title: SA Liberals dump candidate who said homosexuality ‘opens up demonic realms’ after initially standing by him
events.description: Party leader Ashton Hurn confirms Carston Woodhouse will not run for Liberals in SA’s state election, but says ‘people are entitled to have their views’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Liberal candidate in South Australia’s upcoming state election has been dumped after his “shocking and extreme” views on abortion, same-sex marriage, gender transitioning and feminism were aired by his Labor rival. The leader of the SA Liberals, Ashton Hurn, on Wednesday stood by Carston Woodhouse , who had been running for the seat of Wright in Adelaide’s north, after his appearances on the evangelical Christian podcast ElijahFire surfaced. Continue reading...

events.title: US war on Iran: 'An American war in desperate search of a strategy'
events.description: FRANCE 24's François Picard is pleased to welcome Dr. Rouzbeh Parsi, Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lund University in Sweden. According to Dr. Parsi, the current political situation in Iran should be approached with caution, too much attention is being paid to the potential rise of Mojtaba Khamenei. Yet the Islamic Republic is not a system built around a single person, especially during a time of war. Decision-making power lies with institutions such as the Revolutionary Guards and the broader security establishment.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US war on Iran: 'An American war in desperate search of a strategy'

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Robodebt was the great test of Australia’s accountability mechanisms – and they failed
events.description: The final report into the Centrelink debt recovery process that wreaked havoc on the vulnerable is not the full-stop many wanted. It has not restored the trust that was so fundamentally broken Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The whistleblower’s message landed just before Christmas. It was 2016, now a distant memory. Continue reading...

events.title: Access to RSS feeds is rejected
events.description: Your large amount of concurrent requests for RSS data causes problems. Use the API or contact ops@reliefweb.int

events.title: Trump's arch plan and the history of gateways
events.description: The proposed 250‑foot arch has sparked debate in Washington. But monumental gateways have shaped politics, memory, and ritual across cultures for millennia.

events.title: Iran women's team lands in Malaysia amid asylum talks
events.description: The Iranian women's football team has split between Malaysia and Australia after their Asian Cup exit. Some players remain in Australia under humanitarian visas, while the rest await a flight home amid safety concerns.

events.title: Senegal passes law doubling penalty for same-sex relations to 10 years in prison
events.description: Senegal’s parliament on Wednesday passed a law doubling the maximum prison term for same-sex relations to 10 years, intensifying a crackdown on the LGBTQ community. The bill, which also criminalizes promoting or financing such relationships, now awaits President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s signature amid reports of dozens of arrests since February.

events.title: Dem lawmaker disputes police bodycam amid reports he said he was above the law: ‘On top’ or ‘on time’
events.description: A Maryland lawmaker slammed reports of his New Year’s Eve custody dispute, claiming he told police he was "on time" for his child's drop-off, not "on top" of the law.

events.title: Sheriff running Nancy Guthrie case responds after report reveals unseemly exit from first policing job
events.description: Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos' resume corrected after news report reveals he allegedly resigned from El Paso Police to avoid termination in 1982.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: José Antonio Kast, the Pinochet fan about to swerve Chile to the far right
events.description: The new president won office by promising to clean up crime, but his background is red rag to a bull for many Just south of Santiago, the tiny rural town of Paine is a quiet grid of painted adobe facades, shaded squares and shuttered shop fronts as the summer holidays draw to a close. But the white-knuckle fear of crime that propelled its most famous son, José Antonio Kast, to a resounding victory in December’s presidential election is as present in sleepy Paine as it is the length of Chile. Continue reading...

events.title: Chile turns right: Kast inaugurated as nation's most conservative leader since Pinochet
events.description: Chile has sworn in its most right-wing president in decades — and his rise, and ideology, are rooted in a small town beneath the Andes.

events.title: Florida mom accused of abandoning daughter at Universal Studios to go drinking at bar
events.description: A Florida mother was arrested and charged with child neglect after allegedly leaving her 12-year-old daughter alone at a Universal CityWalk bar while she went drinking elsewhere, authorities said.

events.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
events.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

events.title: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
events.description: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.

events.title: Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind
events.description: One of the seven women who received visas has reversed her decision after speaking to teammates, minister says

events.title: Why Namibia's green energy dream could be a red flag for penguins
events.description: A near pristine desert and coastal wilderness in Namibia could soon host a huge hydrogen production facility.

events.title: A small US grocer is calling out the lower prices at big chains
events.description: It is 'impossible for us to compete', says the boss of a New York grocery store.

events.title: Bangladesh parliament meets after uprising, elections ushered in new gov’t
events.description: Newly elected PM Rahman has blamed Sheikh Hasina and her Awami League party for undermining the previous parliament.

events.title: ‘Life covered in soot’: Gas shortage forces Gaza families to cook over wood
events.description: Cooking gas shortages in Gaza force families to burn wood and plastic, exposing mothers and children to dangerous smoke.

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: El Salvador’s mass arrest policy may have led to crimes against humanity, study shows
events.description: Experts documented murder, torture and disappearances under Nayib Bukele’s policy targeting gangs The draconian mass incarceration policy of El Salvador’s president, Nayib Bukele, may have led to crimes against humanity, according to a new study by legal experts. By locking up 1.4% of the population without due process, Bukele turned El Salvador from one of Latin America’s most violent countries into one of its least violent – but at the cost of human rights and the rule of law. Continue reading...

events.title: No Nobles Day: Britain's Parliament boots its last hereditary Lords after 700 years
events.description: Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.

events.title: 'Elon Musk Unveiled: The Tesla Experiment': When unchecked ambition costs lives
events.description: A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars, while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars.

events.title: WAFCON 2026: A 'combination of factors' behind postponement
events.description: African football's governing body CAF says it made the decision to postpone it's top women's tournament to ensure "the best possible conditions." It's not the first time the tournament has been moved at short notice.

events.title: Senegal lawmakers back tougher anti-LGBTQ+ law with up to 10 years in prison
events.description: Parliament approved legislation doubling prison sentences and increasing fines for same-sex relations. Activists say the move reflects a broader push for stricter anti-LGBT laws in the region.

events.title: How Poland is flexing its economic muscle in Western Europe
events.description: Poland's economy is now a European star performer. A new trend suggests further momentum, with more and more Polish firms acquiring foreign companies, including in Germany.

events.title: Rohingya risk lives at sea to flee Myanmar, Bangladesh
events.description: Every year, thousands of Rohingya attempt to flee Myanmar and Bangladesh by sea. The escape effort kills hundreds of people per year.

events.title: Chile marks sharp turn to the right as new hardline president vows sweeping reforms
events.description: Right wing hardliner Jose Antonio Kast was sworn in as Chile’s president Wednesday, marking the nation’s most stark shift to the right since since the brutal dictatorship of general Augusto Pinochet, whom Kast greatly admires. Kast vowed in his inaugural address to follow through on sweeping reforms, including building physical border barriers along the frontier with Bolivia.

events.title: Champions League: PSG pull away late to beat Chelsea (5-2)
events.description: After twice being caught up by an ambitious Blues side, Paris Saint-Germain finally won 5-2, putting one foot in the Champions League quarter-finals. Scoring three goals in the last quarter of an hour, PSG seem to have restored the confidence they displayed last season. They have five days to rest before travelling to London for the second leg.

events.title: PSG stun Chelsea as Jorgensen blunder sparks Champions League thriller
events.description: Paris St Germain beat Chelsea 5-2 in a thrilling Champions League last-16 first leg on Wednesday, helped by a costly error from goalkeeper Filip Jorgensen and two late goals from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia. The sides had been level at 2-2 after Chelsea twice came from behind at the Parc des Princes.

events.title: US energy secretary deletes post claiming Navy escorted tanker through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A brief social media post by the US energy secretary prompted a major U-turn from the Trump administration. Within 30 minutes, Chris Wright had posted – and deleted – a message claiming the US Navy had successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite its short life, the claim sent oil markets into a frenzy, prompted a denial from the White House and triggered mockery from Iranian officials. Vedika Bahl explains in Truth or Fake.

events.title: Van driven into barricade near White House, police say
events.description: A van has been driven into a barricade near the White House, officials have said.

events.title: British couple drown at popular Australian beach
events.description: A British married couple have drowned at a popular beach on the New South Wales coast in Australia.
events.ticker_label: 💥 British couple drown at popular Australian beach

events.title: Senegal lawmakers approve new bill toughening punishment for homosexual acts
events.description: Senegal’s parliament has approved a law that toughens punishment for homosexuality in the largely conservative West African nation

events.title: China adopts an ethnic unity law that critics say will cement assimilation
events.description: China has adopted a sweeping ethnic unity law that critics say tightens assimilation and weakens minority rights

events.title: Romania’s top defense body approves US request to increase its use of airbases to aid Middle East campaign
events.description: Romania’s top defense body approves US request to increase its use of airbases to aid Middle East campaign

events.title: Britain is ejecting hereditary nobles from Parliament after 700 years
events.description: Britain is ending the centuries-old tradition of hereditary aristocrats sitting in Parliament’s House of Lords

events.title: Former scandal-plagued Illinois 'super mayor' eyes political comeback as a Republican in Georgia
events.description: Scandal-plagued former Illinois Mayor Tiffany Henyard switches parties, runs as Republican in Georgia after alleged corruption probe and FBI subpoenas.

events.title: Washington parents demand action, answers after teacher allegedly raped student on school grounds
events.description: A Skykomish K-12 physical education teacher and athletic director has been arrested on suspicion of sexually abusing a student for more than a year.

events.title: Former Kentucky cheerleader indicted on manslaughter charge after newborn son found dead in closet
events.description: Former University of Kentucky student-athlete accused of manslaughter and evidence tampering after newborn found dead in closet, indicted on felonies.

events.title: Disturbing admissions of serial child molester approved for release spur California lawmakers to act
events.description: Convicted child predator who admitted ongoing attraction to girls pleads not guilty to new charge, halting parole as California Republicans push to overhaul elderly parole law.

events.title: Beloved 75-year-old math teacher found dead inside Baltimore elementary school
events.description: A 75-year-old Maryland math teacher who died over the weekend was discovered inside her Baltimore elementary school, with police saying there were no signs of foul play.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Beloved 75-year-old math teacher found dead inside Baltimore elementary school
